questions.text: According to the chapter, what percentage of visual perception is based on the input of visual stimuli received by the eye?
questions.explanation: Visual perception is 80% memory/cognitive function and only 20% based on actual stimuli received by the eye.

questions.text: In visual communication, what is defined as the 'tangible essence of work' including stories, ideas, and information?
questions.explanation: Content is the tangible essence (stories/ideas), while Form is the compositional arrangement of that content.

questions.text: Which design element is considered a 'one-dimensional object' because it only possesses width?
questions.explanation: A dot has zero dimension, a line is one-dimensional (width), and a shape is two-dimensional.

questions.text: The Golden Ratio, often used in design for divine proportion, is represented by the numerical constant Phi (\(\Phi\)). What is its value?
questions.explanation: The numerical constant Phi is approximately 1.61803.

questions.text: A designer is creating a billboard advertisement. Which aspect ratio and orientation is most appropriate based on the text?
questions.explanation: Landscape orientation (wider viewable area) is used for billboards and TV.

questions.text: What type of line should a designer use to promote a sense of power, strength, and grandeur?
questions.explanation: Vertical lines represent standing up to move, promoting power and strength, whereas horizontal lines represent rest and peace.

questions.text: What is the primary effect of using the 'Dutch tilt' (canted angle) in a composition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It creates a sense of peace and tranquility.

choices.label: B
choices.text: It provides a strong visual framework via intersection.

choices.label: C
choices.text: It destabilizes the image to create movement and tension.

choices.label: D
choices.text: It ensures the horizon is perfectly parallel to the frame.

questions.explanation: The Dutch tilt tilts the camera so the horizon is not parallel, which destabilizes the image and creates tension/movement.

questions.text: Which of the following describes the difference between Shape and Form?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Shape is 2D (no depth); Form adds depth through shading and light.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Shape is organic; Form is always geometrical.

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is no difference; they are synonymous.

questions.explanation: Shape is a 2D enclosure of dots/lines, whereas Form adds depth (3D representation) through light, shading, and contrast.

questions.text: According to the RGB color model, which color is produced by mixing Red and Blue?
questions.explanation: In the additive RGB model: Blue + Red = Magenta.

questions.text: Why is the CMYK color model referred to as a 'Subtractive Model'?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Because it removes pixels from the screen.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Because pigments in inks and dyes absorb light waves.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Because it results in a brighter image when colors are added.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Because it is only used for digital light-emitting diodes.

questions.explanation: It is subtractive because pigments absorb (subtract) light waves rather than emitting them like light-based models.

questions.text: In a digital program like Photoshop, if you set the brightness value of a pixel to 0 in a grayscale image, what color will it be?
questions.explanation: In grayscale, brightness ranges from 0 (black) to 255 (white).

questions.text: Which color scheme consists of three colors that are equally spaced apart on the color wheel (e.g., RGB or CMY)?
questions.explanation: A triadic color scheme uses three colors equally spaced apart, resulting in high contrast and vibrancy.

questions.text: Based on the provided text's specific definitions, what is produced when you add white to a hue?
questions.explanation: According to the provided text: 'Adding white produces a brighter hue known as color shade.' (Note: This is the reverse of traditional color theory, but is based on the provided material).

questions.text: What color region is the human eye attracted to first?
questions.explanation: The human eye is attracted to the warm color region (shades of Red, Orange, etc.) first.

questions.text: The '60-30-10' decorating rule suggests what percentage should be allocated to the Accent color?
questions.explanation: The rule is 60% Primary, 30% Secondary, and 10% Accent.
